doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Uh, hi, Dr. Lee. So, um, I fell while playing soccer yesterday, and now I have this really sharp pain in my right hip and groin. It started right after the fall, and it hurts a lot, like, an 8 out of 10, I guess? It’s hard to stand or move without it hurting.

doctor_turn: How would you describe the pain? Is it sharp, dull, or something else?
patient_turn: Oh, it’s definitely sharp, like, really intense. I mean, it feels worse when I try to move or put weight on it, so, um, it’s hard to walk without limping. Honestly, it’s pretty uncomfortable.

doctor_turn: What were you doing when the pain started?
patient_turn: I was, um, playing soccer, just running around and then I fell onto the grass. It wasn’t a big fall or anything, but, like, I landed on my hip, and that’s when the pain hit.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the pain after the fall?
patient_turn: Oh, it was, um, right after the fall, but I didn’t really notice it until a few hours later. I mean, I just thought I was fine at first.

doctor_turn: How has the pain changed since it first started?
patient_turn: Well, um, it hasn’t really gotten any better, to be honest. It’s still really sharp and, like, it hurts just as much as it did at first. I’m kinda worried because I can’t really move my leg properly.

doctor_turn: Where exactly is the pain located? Does it stay in one spot or move around?
Do you have any previous medical conditions or surgeries I should know about?
patient_turn: Um, no, I don’t have any past medical or surgical history. I’ve been pretty healthy, actually. I’m just, like, worried about this pain now.

doctor_turn: Are you currently taking any medications regularly?
patient_turn: No, I’m not taking any regular medications. I haven’t needed anything, you know? Just, um, dealing with this pain now.

doctor_turn: Do you have any allergies to medications or anything else?
patient_turn: Uh, no, I don’t have any known allergies. I’ve never had any issues with medications or anything like that.

doctor_turn: Are your immunizations up to date?
patient_turn: Yeah, I’m up to date on all my immunizations. My parents make sure of that, so, um, I don’t have to worry about it.

doctor_turn: Was your birth and early development normal, with no complications?
patient_turn: Yeah, um, everything was normal with my birth and early development. There weren’t any complications, and I hit all my growth milestones on time. So, um, that’s good, I guess.

doctor_turn: Does anyone in your family have any musculoskeletal conditions?
patient_turn: No, um, there’s no family history of musculoskeletal or rheumatologic conditions. I think everyone’s been pretty healthy, so, um, that’s not something I’m worried about.

doctor_turn: Who do you live with at home?
patient_turn: I live with both my parents and my sibling. It’s, um, a pretty normal home life, I guess. I feel safe there, so that’s good.

doctor_turn: Do you use tobacco, alcohol, or any recreational drugs?
patient_turn: No, I don’t use any tobacco, alcohol, or recreational drugs. I’m not really into that stuff, you know? Just focusing on, um, feeling better from this pain.

doctor_turn: Do you feel safe at home and school?
patient_turn: Yeah, I feel safe at home and at school. There’s, um, no bullying or anything like that, so I guess that’s a relief. Just, you know, worried about this pain right now.

doctor_turn: Have you ever experienced any bullying at school or anywhere else?
patient_turn: No, um, I haven’t experienced any bullying at school or anywhere else. I’ve been pretty lucky that way, I guess. Just, uh, dealing with this pain is my main concern right now.
